title: Driver Teams — My Jobs Visibility
tech: Node.js, TypeScript, Express, PostgreSQL, Prisma, JWT, Zod, React 18, Vite
keyConstraints: TypeScript strict, server-side validation with Zod, Prisma for all DB queries, CSS Modules only (no inline styles)
description: Team members see team-assigned jobs in their my-jobs view, in addition to their individually assigned jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/jobs/my-jobs returns jobs where assignedDriverId = me OR teamId is in teams the driver belongs to (via TeamMember records)
Team-assigned jobs display correctly in MyJobsPage with all existing fields (status, schedule, address)
Driver can transition team job status (ASSIGNED → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED) via PATCH /api/jobs/:id/status
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
implementationNotes: My-jobs query: first fetch driver's team IDs — const memberships = await prisma.teamMember.findMany({ where: { userId }, select: { teamId: true } }); const teamIds = memberships.map(m => m.teamId)
Then query jobs: where: { deletedAt: null, OR: [{ assignedDriverId: userId }, { teamId: { in: teamIds } }] }
Status transition auth: allow PATCH /api/jobs/:id/status if job.assignedDriverId = userId OR job.teamId is in driver's teamIds — same lookup pattern
Frontend: no changes expected if MyJobsPage already maps over the jobs array — verify the job card renders teamId assignment correctly (show team name if assignedDriverId is null and teamId is set)
